basic_details.job_title: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 61 Peon, Typist, Stenographer Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Chikkamagaluru has released five separate notifications inviting online applications to fill 61 vacancies for the posts of Peon, Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, Typist-Copyist, and Process Server.

application_fee.details: The fee must be paid online through State Bank Collect using Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI. Demand drafts, cheques, or postal orders will not be accepted, and the fee once paid is non-refundable.

age_limit.details: Category Age Limit General 18 to 35 years (+5 years relaxation as a one-time measure) Category 2A/2B/3A/3B 18 to 38 years (+5 years relaxation) SC/ST/Category-1 18 to 40 years (+5 years relaxation) | Additional age relaxation applies for government employees, widows, ex-servicemen, armed forces personnel, and persons with disabilities, as per applicable rules.

qualification.details: Eligibility differs by post, and candidates should check both the educational qualification and age limit applicable to the specific position they are applying for. Meeting both criteria is mandatory to be considered for the selection process. | Additional age relaxation applies for government employees, widows, ex-servicemen, armed forces personnel, and persons with disabilities, as per applicable rules.

salary_details.details: Pay scales follow the Karnataka government pay matrix and vary by post. | All posts also include other allowances admissible as per government rules.

selection_process: Selection happens through a multi-stage process that combines written tests
and interviews depending on the post applied for. The qualifying criteria and shortlisting ratios differ slightly by post.
Written/Qualifying Test: Conducted for most posts based on the prescribed syllabus. Skill Test: For Typist
a typing/shorthand skill test on computer is conducted. For example

and shorthand dictation of 80 words per minute transcribed in 45 minutes for Stenographer Grade-III. Interview: Candidates shortlisted from the qualifying exam are called for interview in a ratio of 1:5 (one vacancy to five candidates) for most posts

and 1:25 for Process Server (final list based on 1:5 shortlisting). Document Verification: Original certificates are verified at the time of the skill test/interview. Final Merit List: Prepared based on combined marks from the qualifying test
subject to securing minimum qualifying marks.
how_to_apply: Applications can only be submitted online, and corrections after fee payment are not allowed. Candidates should follow the steps below carefully:
Go to the Notice Category > Recruitments section and select the relevant post link.
Read all instructions in the notification carefully before filling the form.
Register using a valid mobile number and email ID (mandatory, as all communication is sent via SMS/email).
Fill in all required details accurately in the online application form.
Upload a recent colour photograph (5cm x 3.6cm, max 50kb, JPG format, white background).
Upload a signature (2.5cm x 7.5cm, max 25kb, JPG format), made with black or blue ink on white paper.
Pay the prescribed application fee online via State Bank Collect.
Download and print the submitted application and fee payment receipt for future reference and for producing at the time of the exam.
Submit the application before 28 July 2026, 11:59 PM - late or offline applications will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Overview
content_sections.text: The Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Chikkamagaluru has released five separate notifications inviting online applications to fill 61 vacancies for the posts of Peon, Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, Typist-Copyist, and Process Server.

rows.information: Office of the Principal District and Sessions Judge, Chikkamagaluru
Peon, Typist, Stenographer Grade-III, Process Server, Typist-Copyist

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: The exact dates for the written/skill test and interview have not yet been announced. These will be communicated separately to eligible candidates via SMS and email, so candidates must keep their registered mobile number and email ID active and correct.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Breakdown (Post-wise and Category-wise)
content_sections.text: The 61 vacancies are spread across five separate notifications, each carrying its own category-wise reservation. This single post is reserved for the SC category.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Eligibility differs by post, and candidates should check both the educational qualification and age limit applicable to the specific position they are applying for. Meeting both criteria is mandatory to be considered for the selection process. Additional age relaxation applies for government employees, widows, ex-servicemen, armed forces personnel, and persons with disabilities, as per applicable rules.

rows.qualification_required: SSLC or its equivalent examination; should be able to read and write Kannada
SSLC or equivalent; possession of a valid driving licence will be given preference
Second PUC or equivalent, along with Junior Grade certificate in Kannada and English typewriting from the Department of Public Instruction
Second PUC or a three-year Diploma in Commercial Practice, along with Senior Grade typewriting certificate in Kannada and English
Second PUC along with Senior Grade Shorthand in Kannada, plus Senior Grade Typewriting certificates in both Kannada and English

rows.age_limit: 18 to 35 years (+5 years relaxation as a one-time measure)

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Pay scales follow the Karnataka government pay matrix and vary by post. All posts also include other allowances admissible as per government rules.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The fee must be paid online through State Bank Collect using Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI. Demand drafts, cheques, or postal orders will not be accepted, and the fee once paid is non-refundable.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection happens through a multi-stage process that combines written tests, skill or typing tests, and interviews depending on the post applied for. The qualifying criteria and shortlisting ratios differ slightly by post. Written/Qualifying Test: Conducted for most posts based on the prescribed syllabus. Skill Test: For Typist, Typist-Copyist, and Stenographer posts, a typing/shorthand skill test on computer is conducted. For example, a 15-minute dictation for Typist-Copyist, and shorthand dictation of 80 words per minute transcribed in 45 minutes for Stenographer Grade-III. Interview: Candidates shortlisted from the qualifying exam are called for interview in a ratio of 1:5 (one vacancy to five candidates) for most posts, 1:10 for Peon, and 1:25 for Process Server (final list based on 1:5 shortlisting). Document Verification: Original certificates are verified at the time of the skill test/interview. Final Merit List: Prepared based on combined marks from the qualifying test, skill test, and interview, subject to securing minimum qualifying marks.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Probation Period and Service Conditions
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be on probation for two years as per the Karnataka Civil Services (Probation) Rules, 1977, extendable if required. During this period, candidates must pass departmental examinations in their subject as well as the Kannada language examination, and where applicable, the Compulsory Kannada Typewriting Test as per rules.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: Document Requirements
content_sections.text: Applicants claiming reservation under category, rural, ex-serviceman, widow, or PwD quotas must produce valid certificates issued by competent authorities. General category candidates claiming rural reservation must submit an affidavit in Annexure-1 along with the certificate in Annexure-2. All such certificates must be obtained on or before the last date of application (28 July 2026), or any later date specified by competent authorities. Government/PSU employees applying must mention this in the application and produce a No Objection Certificate from their department if selected.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026: How to Apply Online
content_sections.text: Applications can only be submitted online, and corrections after fee payment are not allowed. Candidates should follow the steps below carefully: Visit the official website: chikkamagaluru.dcourts.gov.in. Go to the Notice Category > Recruitments section and select the relevant post link. Read all instructions in the notification carefully before filling the form. Register using a valid mobile number and email ID (mandatory, as all communication is sent via SMS/email). Fill in all required details accurately in the online application form. Upload a recent colour photograph (5cm x 3.6cm, max 50kb, JPG format, white background). Upload a signature (2.5cm x 7.5cm, max 25kb, JPG format), made with black or blue ink on white paper. Pay the prescribed application fee online via State Bank Collect. Download and print the submitted application and fee payment receipt for future reference and for producing at the time of the exam. Submit the application before 28 July 2026, 11:59 PM - late or offline applications will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Many applicants face rejection or delays due to avoidable errors during the application process. Being aware of these common pitfalls in advance can help candidates submit a clean, error-free application. Incomplete or incorrect mobile/email details: Since all communication regarding shortlisting is sent only via SMS and email, an incorrect entry means missing important updates entirely. Wrong photo/signature specifications: Uploading images that don't match the exact size (5cm x 3.6cm for photo, 2.5cm x 7.5cm for signature) and file size limits (50kb/25kb) can lead to application errors. Applying for multiple categories in one form: Candidates must apply separately if eligible under different reservation categories, but should clearly mention only the category they are claiming in the relevant application. Paying fee without final review: Since fees are non-refundable and applications cannot be corrected after submission, candidates should double-check all entered details before making payment. Missing certificate cut-off dates: Reservation, rural quota, or PwD certificates obtained after the last date of application may not be considered valid for that quota. Submitting applications offline: Only online applications through the official portal are accepted; applications sent by post, email, or any other mode will be rejected.

content_sections.heading: Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 28 July 2026, up to 11:59 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are available in this recruitment?
faq.answer: There are 61 total vacancies across Peon (47), Typist (6), Stenographer Grade-III (5), Process Server (2), and Typist-Copyist (1).

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Peon post?
faq.answer: Candidates need to have passed SSLC or equivalent and should be able to read and write Kannada.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for general category candidates?
faq.answer: General category candidates must pay ₹300, while Category 2A/2B/3A/3B candidates pay ₹150; SC/ST/Category-1/PwD candidates are exempted.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit to apply?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years; the maximum is 35 years for General, 38 years for 2A/2B/3A/3B, and 40 years for SC/ST/Category-1, with additional 5-year relaxation as a one-time measure.

faq.question: Q6. How can I apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only through chikkamagaluru.dcourts.gov.in under the Notice Category > Recruitments section.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Chikkamagaluru District Court Recruitment 2026?
